_min_score_comment: Conservative floor; raise to 0.92 once extraction is wired in Phase 0 final and we have a real baseline number to anchor on.
_benefitYear_comment: All 7 product sheets carry Period of Insurance 01/05/2026 - 30/04/2027. The Renewal Overall Premium summary sheet repeats the same dates. No multi-year ambiguity here.
_insurers_comment: Tokio Marine Life carries 5 products (GTL/GHS/GP/SP/Dental); Allied World carries 2 (GPA/WICA). Allied World is NOT in the current seed catalogue — extractor should still emit the code or surface an unknown-insurer warning. Adding ALLIED_WORLD to the catalogue is a Phase 1 follow-up.

products._plans_comment: GHS uses Plan 1 with cover-tier rates (EO=360 per employee). 360 x 2 EO = 720. The schedule per cover tier is per-employee per-year; same situation as [REDACTED] GHS — Phase 1 introduces basis='per_employee'.
GCGP (Group Clinical General Practitioner) maps to GP product code. Plan 1, per-employee rate 311, headcount 2 -> 622 declared.
GCSP (Group Clinical Specialist Insurance) maps to SP product code. Plan 2, per-employee rate 163, headcount 2 -> 326 declared.
GD (Group Dental) maps to Dental product code. Plan 1, per-employee rate 182, headcount 2 -> 364 declared.

products._declaredPremium_comment: Mismatch: summary sheet says 200, product sheet's annual premium row says 50 (computed = 0.0005 * 100k SI = 50). Likely a min-premium clause not stated on the slip. Reconciliation tests should accept either; we set declaredPremium=200 (the summary sheet wins) and note the discrepancy in expectedWarnings.

Slip explicitly says 'MIN. PREMIUM : SGD250.00 before GST'. Computed wages-x-rate is ~163 (60000*0.00042 + 55000*0.0025 = 25.2 + 137.5). Min-premium clause is the canonical example of why we cannot enforce computed==declared.
products._plans_comment: Two classes (Class 1 = administrative, Class 2 = manual/driver). Each class is a separate premium rate.

reconciliation._grand_comment: Grand declared = 130 + 720 + 622 + 326 + 364 + 200 + 250 = 2612 (matches Renewal Overall Premium total). Computed sum is 130 + 720 + 622 + 326 + 364 + 50 + 162.7 = 2374.7 because GPA and WICA both have premium-floor mismatches. Allow wide range (5%) on grandComputed.

_expectedWarnings_comment: AI should surface these as workbook-level warnings when extraction completes. Substring matches; case-insensitive.
